sorting_text.label: Sorting text
sorting_text.summary: Learn how to sort text columns with ORDER BY so product names and categories appear in a clear, predictable order.

sketch1.title: Descending text order and multiple sort columns

project.title: Practice sorting text in product reports

sc-1.title: What ASC means for text
sc-1.prompt: When you use `ORDER BY name ASC` on a text column, how are the rows sorted?
sc-1.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove choices from unrelated topics.
help.hint_2: Choose the option that directly matches the question without relying on answer wording.

options.c: From shortest word to longest word
options.d: In the order the rows were inserted

sc-2.title: Second sort column behavior
sc-2.prompt: In `ORDER BY category ASC, name ASC`, when does SQL use `name` for sorting?
sc-2.hint: The first column decides the main order. The next column helps inside matching first-column values.

help.concept: With multiple columns in `ORDER BY`, SQL sorts by the first column first. It uses the second column only when rows have the same value in the first column.
help.hint_1: Ask what happens when two products share the same category.
help.hint_2: The second sort does not replace the first one; it breaks ties within it.

options.a: Only when two or more rows have the same category
options.b: Only when two or more rows have the same name

mc-1.title: Queries that sort text A to Z
mc-1.prompt: Choose all queries that sort product names in alphabetical order from A to Z.
mc-1.hint: Look for queries that order by the `name` column in ascending order.

help.concept: Text sorting from A to Z uses `ORDER BY` on the text column with `ASC`, or with ascending order left implicit.
help.hint_1: Check whether the query sorts by `name`, not by `category` or `price`.
help.hint_2: One correct query says `ASC` directly, and another leaves ascending order as the default.

options.a: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY name ASC;
options.b: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY name;
options.c: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY name DESC;
options.d: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY category ASC;

mc-2.title: Valid uses of text sorting
mc-2.prompt: Choose all statements that correctly describe sorting text in this lesson.
mc-2.hint: Think about `ASC`, `DESC`, and what happens with more than one sort column.

help.concept: Text columns can be sorted alphabetically with `ASC`, reverse alphabetically with `DESC`, and by multiple columns where later columns break ties from earlier ones.
help.hint_1: Check which statements match alphabetical order and tie-breaking behavior.
help.hint_2: More than one statement is true: one about reverse order and one about multiple columns.

options.a: `ORDER BY name DESC` sorts text from Z to A.
options.b: `ORDER BY category, name` can sort by category first and then by name inside each category.
options.c: `ORDER BY` works only with number columns, not text columns.
options.d: When sorting text, `DESC` means shortest word first.

dr-1.title: Build a basic text-sorting query
dr-1.prompt: Arrange the SQL parts to create a query that shows product names and sorts them from A to Z.
dr-1.hint: Start with SELECT, then FROM, then the ORDER BY clause.

help.concept: A basic SQL query follows the clause order `SELECT`, `FROM`, then `ORDER BY`.
help.hint_1: The column list comes right after `SELECT`.
help.hint_2: The sorting clause belongs at the end of the query.

dr-2.title: Build a two-column sort
dr-2.prompt: Arrange the SQL parts to sort products by category A to Z, then by name Z to A inside each category.
dr-2.hint: The query still starts with SELECT and FROM. The two sort columns belong together in one ORDER BY clause.

help.concept: Multiple sort columns are written in one `ORDER BY` clause, separated by commas, in the order SQL should apply them.
help.hint_1: Put `category ASC` before `name DESC` because category is the main sort.
help.hint_2: Do not split the sorting into two separate `ORDER BY` clauses.

tokens.t3: ORDER BY category ASC, name DESC;

fb-1.title: Fill the sorting direction
fb-1.prompt: Complete the query so product names are sorted from Z to A.
fb-1.hint: Read the sentence around the blank and decide what role the missing word plays.

help.concept: The blank should be filled with the term that makes the statement accurate.
help.hint_1: Look at the words before and after the blank to infer what kind of term is needed.
help.hint_2: Choose the option that completes the statement with the clearest meaning.

fb-1.template: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY name [blank1];

fb-2.title: Fill the second sort column
fb-2.prompt: Complete the query so rows are sorted by category first, then by product name.
fb-2.hint: The second sort column should be the text column that stores each product's title.

help.concept: In a multi-column sort, the second column is written after the comma and is used to order rows that share the same first-column value.
help.hint_1: The first sort column is already `category`.
help.hint_2: Use the column that contains values like `Sketchbook` and `Desk Lamp`.

fb-2.template: SELECT name, category FROM products ORDER BY category ASC, [blank1] ASC;

sketch-1.title: Sorting text with ORDER BY
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
When you sort text in SQL, the database arranges words in alphabetical order. You do this with `ORDER BY`.

A simple example with the `products` table is:

```sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
ORDER BY name ASC;
```

Step by step:
- `SELECT name, category` chooses the columns to show.
- `FROM products` says which table to read.
- `ORDER BY name ASC` sorts the rows by the `name` column from A to Z.

With the sample data, names would begin like `Acrylic Paint Set`, `Desk Lamp`, `Fountain Pen`, and so on.

`ASC` means ascending order. For text, that usually means A to Z. If you leave `ASC` out, SQL usually sorts ascending by default.

sketch-2.title: Descending text order and multiple sort columns
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown:
You can also sort text in reverse alphabetical order with `DESC`.

```sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
ORDER BY name DESC;
```

This sorts product names from Z to A.

You can sort by more than one column too. SQL uses the first sort column first, then uses the next one to break ties.

```sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
ORDER BY category ASC, name ASC;
```

Step by step:
- First, rows are grouped in alphabetical order by `category`.
- Inside each category, rows are sorted by `name`.

Using the sample data, the `Art` products would appear together, and inside that group `Acrylic Paint Set` would come before `Sketchbook`.
